to.p: make an order—

ul.li: (a) placing the child with respect to whom the application is made in the care of a designated local authority;
or (b) putting him under the supervision of a designated local authority . . ..

condition.p: receiving the application of any local authority or authorised person

to.p: make a care order or supervision order

condition.p: it is satisfied—

ul.li: (a)that the child concerned is suffering,
or is likely to suffer,
significant harm;
and (b)that the harm,
or likelihood of harm,
is attributable to— (i)the care given to the child,
or likely to be given to him if the order were not made,
not being what it would be reasonable to expect a parent to give to him;
or (ii)the child’s being beyond parental control.

to.p: be made with respect to a child who has reached the age of seventeen (or sixteen, in the case of a child who is married).
consider the permanence provisions of the section 31A plan for the child concerned, but

condition.p: deciding whether to make a care order.

to.p: not consider the remainder of the section 31A plan, subject to section 34(11).

condition.p: deciding whether to make a care order.

to.p: by regulations, amend this section for the purpose of altering what for the purposes of subsection (3A) are the permanence provisions of a section 31A plan.
be made on its own or in any other family proceedings.

ul.li: (a) on an application for a care order,
make a supervision order;
(b) on an application for a supervision order,
make a care order.

to.p: consult the local authority appearing to him to be the authority in whose area the child concerned is ordinarily resident

condition.p: the authorised person proposes to make an application under this section
it is reasonably practicable to do so
making the application

for.p: An application made by an authorised person

to.p: be entertained by the court

condition.p: at the time when it is made, the child concerned is—

ul.li: (a) the subject of an earlier application for a care order,
or supervision order,
which has not been disposed of;
or (b) subject to— (i) a care order or supervision order;
(ii) a youth rehabilitation order within the meaning given by section 173 of the Sentencing Code;
or (iii) a compulsory supervision order or interim compulsory supervision order as defined by sections 83 and 86 of the Children’s Hearings (Scotland) Act 2011.

for.p: The local authority designated in a care order

to.p: be—

ul.li: (a)the authority within whose area the child is ordinarily resident;
or (b)where the child does not reside in the area of a local authority,
the authority within whose area any circumstances arose in consequence of which the order is being made.

to.p: be compared with that which could reasonably be expected of a similar child

condition.p: the question of whether harm suffered by the child is significant turns on the child’s health or development.

to.p: within such time as the court may direct, prepare a plan (“ a care plan ”) for the future care of the child

condition.p: an application is made on which a care order might be made with respect to a child

to.p: keep any care plan prepared by them under review and, if they are of the opinion some change is required, revise the plan, or make a new plan, accordingly

condition.p: the application is pending.

to.p: give any prescribed information and do so in the prescribed manner.

for.p: A court in which an application for an order under this Part is proceeding

to.p: (in the light of any provision in rules of court that is of the kind mentioned in subsection (2)

ul.li: (a) draw up a timetable with a view to disposing of the application— (i) without delay,
and (ii) in any event within twenty-six weeks beginning with the day on which the application was issued;
and (b) give such directions as it considers appropriate for the purpose of ensuring,
so far as is reasonably practicable,
that that timetable is adhered to.
(a) specify periods within which specified steps must be taken in relation to such proceedings;
and (b) make other provision with respect to such proceedings for the purpose of ensuring,
so far as is reasonably practicable,
that they are disposed of without delay.

to.p: in particular have regard to—

ul.li: (a) the impact which the timetable would have on the welfare of the child to whom the application relates;
and (b) the impact which the timetable would have on the conduct of the proceedings

condition.p: drawing up a timetable under subsection (1)

to.p: in particular have regard to—

ul.li: (a) the impact which any revision would have on the welfare of the child to whom the application relates;
and (b) the impact which any revision would have on the duration and conduct of the proceedings

condition.p: revising a timetable drawn up under subsection (1)

ul.li: (a) or when making any decision which may give rise to a need to revise such a timetable (which does not include a decision under subsection (5)).

for.p: A court in which an application under this Part is proceeding

to.p: extend the period that is for the time being allowed under subsection (1)

ul.li: (a)(ii) in the case of the application,

condition.p: the court considers that the extension is necessary to enable the court to resolve the proceedings justly.

to.p: in particular have regard to—

ul.li: (a) the impact which any ensuing timetable revision would have on the welfare of the child to whom the application relates,
and (b) the impact which any ensuing timetable revision would have on the duration and conduct of the proceedings;

condition.p: deciding whether to grant an extension under subsection (5),

to.p: take account of the following guidance: extensions are not to be granted routinely and are to be seen as requiring specific justification

condition.p: deciding whether to grant an extension under subsection (5).

to.p: by regulations amend subsection (1)

ul.li: or the opening words of subsection (8),
for the purpose of varying the period for the time being specified in that provision.

to.p: provide that a court—

ul.li: (a) when deciding whether to exercise the power under subsection (5),
or (b) when deciding how to exercise that power,
must,
or may or may not,
have regard to matters specified in the rules,
or must take account of any guidance set out in the rules.

for.p: the local authority designated by the order

to.p: receive the child into their care and to keep him in their care while the order remains in force

condition.p: the care order is made with respect to a child.

to.p: be kept in the care of an authorised person until received into the care of the authority.

ul.li: (a) a care order has been made with respect to a child on the application of that person;
but (b) the local authority designated by the order was not informed that that person proposed to make the application

for.p: the local authority designated by the order

ul.li: (a) have parental responsibility for the child;
and (b) have the power (subject to the following provisions of this section) to determine the extent to which — (i) a parent,
guardian or special guardian of the child;
or (ii) a person who by virtue of section 4A has parental responsibility for the child,
may meet his parental responsibility for him

condition.p: a care order is in force with respect to a child.

for.p: the local authority designated by a care order

to.p: not exercise the power in subsection (3)(b)

condition.p: they are satisfied that it is necessary to do so in order to safeguard or promote the child’s welfare.

for.p: a person mentioned in that provision who has care of the child

to.p: do what is reasonable in all the circumstances of the case for the purpose of safeguarding or promoting his welfare.

for.p: the local authority designated by a care order in force with respect to a child

ul.li: (a) cause the child to be brought up in any religious persuasion other than that in which he would have been brought up if the order had not been made;
or (b) have the right— (ii) to agree or refuse to agree to the making of an adoption order,
or an order under section 84 of the Adoption and Children Act 2002,
with respect to the child;
or (iii) to appoint a guardian for the child.
(a) cause a child to be known by a new surname;
or (b) remove him from the United Kingdom,
without either the written consent of every person who has parental responsibility for the child or the leave of the court

condition.p: a care order is in force with respect to the child.

to.p: allow a child reasonable contact with—

ul.li: (a) his parents;
(b) any guardian or special guardian of his;

condition.p: the child is in the care of a local authority
the provisions of this section and their duty under section 22(3)

ul.li: (a) or,
where the local authority is in Wales,
under section 78(1)

to.p: make such order as it considers appropriate with respect to the contact which is to be allowed between the child and any named person

condition.p: receiving an application made by the authority or the child.

to.p: make such order as it considers appropriate with respect to the contact which is to be allowed between the child and that person.

condition.p: receiving an application made by—

ul.li: (a) any person mentioned in paragraphs

to.p: make an order authorising the authority to refuse to allow contact between the child and any person who is mentioned in paragraphs

ul.li: (a) to (d) of subsection (1) and named in the order

condition.p: receiving an application made by the authority or the child.

to.p: make an order under this section, even though no application for such an order has been made with respect to the child, if it considers that the order should be made

condition.p: making a care order with respect to a child, or in any family proceedings in connection with a child who is in the care of a local authority.

to.p: refuse to allow the contact that would otherwise be required by virtue of subsection (1) or an order under this section

ul.li: (a) they are satisfied that it is necessary to do so in order to safeguard or promote the child’s welfare;
and (b) the refusal— (i) is decided upon as a matter of urgency;
and (ii) does not last for more than seven days.

to.p: impose such conditions as the court considers appropriate.
by regulations make provision as to— (za) what a local authority in England must have regard to in considering whether contact between a child and a person mentioned in any of paragraphs

ul.li: (a) to (d) of subsection (1) is consistent with safeguarding and promoting the child's welfare;

to.p: vary or discharge any order made under this section on the application of the authority, the child concerned or the person named in the order.
be made either at the same time as the care order itself or later.

ul.li: (a) consider the arrangements which the authority have made,
or propose to make,
for affording any person contact with a child to whom this section applies;
and (b) invite the parties to the proceedings to comment on those arrangements

condition.p: making, varying or discharging an order under this section or making a care order with respect to any child.

to.p: While a supervision order is in force,

ul.li: (a) advise,
assist and befriend the supervised child;
(b) take such steps as are reasonably necessary to give effect to the order;
and (c) where— (i) the order is not wholly complied with;
or (ii) the supervisor considers that the order may no longer be necessary,
consider whether or not to apply to the court for its variation or discharge.

to.p: make an order putting the child with respect to whom the application is made under the supervision of a designated local authority.

condition.p: receiving the application of any local authority.

to.p: make an education supervision order

condition.p: it is satisfied that the child concerned is of compulsory school age and is not being properly educated.

to.p: be made with respect to a child who is in the care of a local authority.
consult the appropriate local authority

condition.p: the local authority propose to make an application for an education supervision order
different.

to.p: direct the appropriate authority to undertake an investigation of the child’s circumstances

condition.p: in any family proceedings in which a question arises with respect to the welfare of any child, it appears to the court that it may be appropriate for a care or supervision order to be made with respect to him.

to.p: consider whether they should—

ul.li: (a) apply for a care order or for a supervision order with respect to the child;
(b) provide services or assistance for the child or his family;
or (c) take any other action with respect to the child

condition.p: undertaking the investigation
the court gives a direction under this section

to.p: inform the court of—

ul.li: (a) their reasons for so deciding;
(b) any service or assistance which they have provided,
or intend to provide,
for the child and his family;
and (c) any other action which they have taken,
or propose to take,
with respect to the child

condition.p: the local authority undertake an investigation under this section, and decide not to apply for a care order or supervision order with respect to the child concerned.

to.p: be given to the court

condition.p: the end of the period of eight weeks beginning with the date of the direction
the court otherwise directs.

to.p: consider whether it would be appropriate to review the case at a later date; and (b) if they decide that it would be, they shall determine the date on which that review is to begin.

condition.p: , on the conclusion of any investigation or review under this section, the authority decide not to apply for a care order or supervision order with respect to the child

to.p: make an interim care order or an interim supervision order with respect to a child

ul.li: (a) in any proceedings on an application for a care order or supervision order,
the proceedings are adjourned;
or (b) the court gives a direction under section 37(1).

to.p: make an interim care order or interim supervision order under this section

condition.p: it is satisfied that there are reasonable grounds for believing that the circumstances with respect to the child are as mentioned in section 31(2).

to.p: make an interim supervision order with respect to a child

condition.p: in any proceedings on an application for a care order or supervision order, the court makes a child arrangements order with respect to the living arrangements of the child concerned
satisfied that his welfare will be satisfactorily safeguarded without an interim order being made.

to.p: give such directions (if any) as it considers appropriate with regard to the medical or psychiatric examination or other assessment of the child;

condition.p: the court makes an interim care order, or interim supervision order,

to.p: refuse to submit to the examination or other assessment

condition.p: the child is of sufficient understanding to make an informed decision.

to.p: be to the effect that there is to be—

ul.li: (a)no such examination or assessment;
or (b)no such examination or assessment

condition.p: the court directs otherwise.

for.p: A direction under subsection (6) to the effect that there is to be a medical or psychiatric examination or other assessment of the child

to.p: be given

condition.p: the court is of the opinion that the examination or other assessment is necessary to assist the court to resolve the proceedings justly.

to.p: have regard in particular to—

ul.li: (a) any impact which any examination or other assessment would be likely to have on the welfare of the child,
and any other impact which giving the direction would be likely to have on the welfare of the child,
(b) the issues with which the examination or other assessment would assist the court,
(c) the questions which the examination or other assessment would enable the court to answer,
(d) the evidence otherwise available,
(e) the impact which the direction would be likely to have on the timetable,
duration and conduct of the proceedings,
(f) the cost of the examination or other assessment,
and (g) any matters prescribed by Family Procedure Rules

condition.p: deciding whether to give a direction under subsection (6) to that effect

to.p: be—

ul.li: (a)given when the interim order is made or at any time while it is in force;
and (b)varied at any time on the application of any person falling within any class of person prescribed by rules of court for the purposes of this subsection.

to.p: consider whether any party who was, or might have been, opposed to the making of the order was in a position to argue his case against the order in full

condition.p: the court makes an order under or by virtue of this section
determining the period for which the order is to be in force.

to.p: include an exclusion requirement in the interim care order

condition.p: Where—

ul.li: (a) on being satisfied that there are reasonable grounds for believing that the circumstances with respect to a child are as mentioned in section 31(2)

to.p: provide that the exclusion requirement is to have effect for a shorter period than the other provisions of the interim care order.
attach a power of arrest to the exclusion requirement

condition.p: the court makes an interim care order containing an exclusion requirement.

to.p: provide that the power of arrest is to have effect for a shorter period than the exclusion requirement

condition.p: the court attaches a power of arrest to an exclusion requirement of an interim care order.

for.p: Any period specified for the purposes of subsection (4) or (6)

to.p: be extended by the court (on one or more occasions)

condition.p: the court receives an application to vary or discharge the interim care order.

to.p: arrest without warrant any person whom he has reasonable cause to believe to be in breach of the requirement

condition.p: a power of arrest is attached to an exclusion requirement of an interim care order by virtue of subsection (5).

to.p: accept an undertaking from the relevant person

condition.p: handling any case where the court has power to include an exclusion requirement in an interim care order.

to.p: be discharged by the court on the application of—

ul.li: (a)any person who has parental responsibility for the child;
(b)the child himself;
or (c)the local authority designated by the order.

to.p: be varied or discharged by the court on the application of—

ul.li: (a)any person who has parental responsibility for the child;
(b)the child himself;
or (c)the supervisor.

to.p: be varied by the court in so far as it imposes a requirement which affects that person

condition.p: the court receives the application of a person who is not entitled to apply for the order to be discharged, but who is a person with whom the child is living.

to.p: be varied or discharged by the court in so far as it imposes the exclusion requirement

condition.p: receiving the application of a person who is not entitled to apply for the order to be discharged, but who is a person to whom an exclusion requirement contained in the order applies.

to.p: vary or discharge an interim care On the application of a person who is not entitled to apply for the order to be discharged, but who is a person to whom an exclusion requirement contained in the order applies, in so far as it imposes the exclusion requirement.
vary or discharge the order in so far as it confers a power of arrest (whether or not any application has been made to vary or discharge any other provision of the order)

condition.p: a power of arrest has been attached to an exclusion requirement of an interim care order
receiving the application of any person entitled to apply for the discharge of the order so far as it imposes the exclusion requirement.

to.p: substitute a supervision order for the care order.

condition.p: a care order is in force with respect to a child
receiving the application of any person entitled to apply for the order to be discharged.

to.p: make a care order with respect to the child to have effect subject to such directions (if any) as the court may see fit to include in the order.

ul.li: (a) the court dismisses an application for a care order;
and (b) at the time when the court dismisses the application,
the child concerned is the subject of an interim care order.

to.p: make a supervision order with respect to the child to have effect subject to such directions (if any) as the court may see fit to include in the order

condition.p: Where—

ul.li: (a) the court dismisses an application for a care order,
or an application for a supervision order;
and (b) at the time when the court dismisses the application,
the child concerned is the subject of an interim supervision order.

to.p: order that

ul.li: (a) its decision is not to have effect;
or (b) the care order,
or supervision order,
is to continue to have effect but subject to such directions as the court sees fit to include in the order

condition.p: the court grants an application to discharge a care order or supervision order.

to.p: extend the period for which the order in question is to have effect, but not so as to extend it beyond the end of the appeal period.

ul.li: (a) an appeal is made against any decision of a court under this section;
or (b) any application is made to the appellate court in connection with a proposed appeal against that decision,

to.p: For the purpose of any specified proceedings, appoint an officer of the Serviceor a Welsh family proceedings officer for the child concerned

condition.p: the court is satisfied that it is not necessary to do so in order to safeguard his interests.

for.p: The officer of the Serviceor Welsh family proceedings officer

ul.li: (a) be appointed in accordance with rules of court;
and (b) be under a duty to safeguard the interests of the child in the manner prescribed by such rules.

to.p: appoint a solicitor to represent a child

ul.li: (a) the child concerned is not represented by a solicitor;
and (b) any of the conditions mentioned in subsection (4) is satisfied,

for.p: Any solicitor appointed under or by virtue of this section

to.p: be appointed, and represent the child, in accordance with rules of court.
make provision as to—

ul.li: (a) the assistance which any officer of the Serviceor Welsh family proceedings officer may be required by the court to give to it;
(b) the consideration to be given by any officer of the Serviceor Welsh family proceedings officer ,
where an order of a specified kind has been made in the proceedings in question,
as to whether to apply for the variation or discharge of the order;
(c) the participation of officers of the Serviceor Welsh family proceedings officers in reviews,
of a kind specified in the rules,
which are conducted by the court.

to.p: Regardless of any enactment or rule of law which would otherwise prevent it from doing so, take account of

ul.li: (a) any statement contained in a report made by an officer of the Serviceor a Welsh family proceedings officer who is appointed under this section for the purpose of the proceedings in question;
and (b) any evidence given in respect of the matters referred to in the report,
in so far as the statement or evidence is,
in the opinion of the court,
relevant to the question which the court is considering.

for.p: an officer of the Service or Welsh family proceedings officer

to.p: have the right at all reasonable times to examine and take copies of—

ul.li: (a) any records of,
or held by,
a local authority or an authorised personwhich were compiled in connection with the making,
or proposed making,
by any person of any application under this Act with respect to the child concerned;
. . . (b) any . . . records of,
or held by,

a local authority which were compiled in connection with any functions which are social services functions within the meaning of the Local Authority Social Services Act 1970 or for the purposes of the Social Services and Well-being (Wales) Act 2014,
so far as those records relate to that child. ;
or (c) any records of,
or held by,
an authorised person which were compiled in connection with the activities of that person,
so far as those records relate to that child

condition.p: the officer of the Service or the Welsh family proceedings officer has been appointed under section 41.

for.p: a copy of any record or any part of it taken by an officer of the Serviceor Welsh family proceedings officer to which he is entitled to examine under this section

to.p: be admissible as evidence of any matter referred to in any—

ul.li: (a) report which he makes to the court in the proceedings in question;
or (b) evidence which he gives in those proceedings.
